news.title: STAT+: Medicare takes another swing at 340B cuts to hospitals
news.summary: Reducing Medicare payments for 340B drugs is the latest proposal focused on the hotly debated drug discount program.

news.title: STAT+: Lawmakers urge HHS to force Eli Lilly to provide 340B drug discounts to hospitals
news.summary: Lawmakers step into a dispute between Eli Lilly and hospitals about the 340b drug discount program.

news.title: STAT+: Elevance sues government over $115 million tied to Medicare Advantage star ratings
news.summary: The health insurer says CMS didn't calculate its star ratings appropriately.

news.title: STAT+: Up and down the ladder: The latest comings and goings
news.summary: Hired someone new and exciting? Promoted a rising star? Finally solved that hard-to-fill spot? Share the news with us, and we’ll share it with others.

news.title: STAT+: What does DeGette’s loss mean for pharma?
news.summary: Colorado Democratic Rep. Diana DeGette, who lost her primary, was knowledgeable about biopharma, if not always a friend to the industry.

news.title: STAT+: Roche drug sets new standard for KRAS-driven lung cancer
news.summary: Biotech's bull market, Vertex's gene therapy expansion, and more news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a court win for Amgen, Novo seeking supplier discounts, and more
news.summary: U.S. judge blocked a Colorado state panel from placing a cap on the price of Amgen's blockbuster drug Enbrel

news.title: STAT+: U.S. judge blocks Colorado’s first-of-its-kind price cap on an Amgen drug
news.summary: A federal judge blocked a Colorado state panel from placing a cap on the price of Enbrel, a blockbuster drug sold by Amgen

news.title: STAT+: More frequent, more intense, and longer heat waves pose health risks for Americans
news.summary: The presidential fitness test is back, surprising new data on the health risks of obesity, and more Morning Rounds news

news.title: STAT+: Halfway into the year, biotech is booming
news.summary: The biotech stock rally that began in April 2025 has not stopped, and if anything, has accelerated.

news.title: STAT+: A ‘historic’ FDA clearance raises the question: Is the LLM an interface or the decision-maker?
news.summary: The newly cleared device is based around an app that helps a patient manage their diabetes using a treatment plan defined by their doctor.

news.title: The new presidential fitness test is a positive step, not the solution, experts say
news.summary: Physical activity needs to be fun to keep kids engaged. And the test risks embarrassing them, instead.

news.title: STAT+: A year after distressed buyout, what’s become of Bluebird Bio?
news.summary: Bluebird Bio was a dark biotech morality tale when David Meek bought it. He says the new company, Genetix, is already making money.

news.title: STAT+: A former AI regulator, now in industry, says biopharma is reading FDA’s guidance wrong
news.summary: Companies are being too conservative in how they interpret FDA's AI guidance, but the agency can do more to help, too, Tala Fakhouri says.

news.title: Opinion: CDC leadership continues to try to manufacture a scientific debate on vaccines where none exists
news.summary: CDC leadership continues to try manufacture a scientific debate on vaccines where none exists, writes Ben Lopman.

news.title: CMS mulls tougher Medicare enrollment rules to combat fraud as part of 2027 home health payment rule
news.summary: CMS is proposing changes to provider enrollment provisions aimed at strengthening the agency's ability to recover improper payments and remove noncompliant providers and suppliers from Medicare.

news.title: CMS goes live with GLP-1 Bridge program for Part D beneficiaries
news.summary: The Trump administration has officially launched its new "bridge" model that brings GLP-1 coverage for weight loss to Medicare beneficiaries.

news.title: Trump administration withholds funds from New York's Medicaid fraud unit
news.summary: As part of its efforts to crack down on fraud in Medicaid, the Trump administration revealed that it will suspend funding for New York's state Medicaid Fraud Control Unit.

news.title: Zelis rolls out AI solution to help payers navigate No Surprises Act dispute process
news.summary: Healthcare technology company Zelis rolled out an AI-native solution to help health plans manage Independent Dispute Resolution complexity under the No Surprises Act.

news.title: Experity acquires Exdion Healthcare to accelerate on-demand care RCM automation
news.summary: The acquisition aims to accelerate RCM automation for on-demand care.

news.title: ACCESS Model: behavioral health edition
news.summary: A new payment model is designed to pay digital health companies based on outcomes in chronic condition management. But where does behavioral health fit? This episode explores that question with SonderMind and Headspace, two companies participating in the model's behavioral health track.

news.title: Saint Peter’s Healthcare System Expands Intelligent Hospital Room Initiative with hellocare.ai to Advance AI Assisted Patient Safety and Virtual Care
news.summary: hellocare.ai is expanding its partnership with Saint Peter's Healthcare System to deploy advanced intelligent hospital room capabilities across the organization.

news.title: Evernorth unveils new AI-powered specialty pharmacy program, Pharmacy Forward
news.summary: Evernorth has unveiled a new specialty pharmacy program that leans on AI to support a better experience for patients with complex conditions.

news.title: 26 states sue CMS over final Medicaid work requirements rule
news.summary: A coalition of 26 states is suing the Trump administration over its rollout of Medicaid work requirements.

news.title: Startup Queue lands $12.6M to launch autonomous robotic pharmacy kiosks
news.summary: Startup Queue emerged from stealth to launch what it calls the first fully autonomous robotic pharmacy in the form of prescription medication kiosks.

news.title: Carbon Health agrees to revise contracts with its California clinics, pay penalties
news.summary: The primary care chain's settlement agreement follows a California DOJ investigation of its advertising and billing practices, as well as contracts that allegedly gave the company de facto control over its physician-owned clinics.

news.title: AI playing a major role in consumers' healthcare decision-making, survey finds
news.summary: Patients are taking charge of their healthcare with artificial intelligence and other solutions, with 58% of U.S. patients researching symptoms before deciding whether to book an appointment, a survey found.

news.title: Can We Outrun Cancer's Evolution? Why BrYet Is Targeting Cancer-Specific Phenotypes
news.summary: Genetic instability makes metastatic cancer largely incurable. BrYet aims to work around it with a new approach targeting cancer-specific phenotypes.

news.title: AstraZeneca pens $1.7B kidney drug discovery pact with go-to Chinese partner CSPC
news.summary: AstraZeneca has added another piece to its sprawling interface with CSPC Pharmaceutical, paying the Chinese biotech $30 million upfront to jointly discover and develop two kidney disease drug candidates.

news.title: United Therapeutics pays $140M for thymic cell therapy startup
news.summary: United Therapeutics has paid $140 million to buy Thymmune Therapeutics for preclinical regenerative cell therapies focused on the thymus.

news.title: Roche orchestrates phase 3 KRAS lung cancer win over Amgen, BMS
news.summary: Roche’s investigational KRAS G12C inhibitor has beaten Amgen’s Lumakras and Bristol Myers Squibb’s Krazati in a phase 3 trial, furthering its ambitions to establish divarasib as a new standard of care.

news.title: Fosun pays $32M to join Lilly and Novartis in fiercely competitive psoriasis market
news.summary: A Fosun Pharma subsidiary has paid Junshi Biosciences 215 million yuan ($32 million) for Chinese rights to a near-approval autoimmune disease drug candidate.

news.title: Insilico keeps partnership sprint apace in $600M biobucks collab with Takeda
news.summary: Insilico Medicine has signed a deal worth up to $600 million with Japanese Big Pharma Takeda to collaborate on discovery of assets across Takeda’s therapeutic areas of interest.

news.title: Merck halts ph. 2 Alzheimer’s trial after Neuphoria small molecule underwhelms
news.summary: Merck & Co. has nixed the phase 2 study of a potential Alzheimer’s disease medicine after the drug underwhelmed in an interim analysis, marking another blow for the pharma’s struggling partner Neuphoria Therapeutics.

news.title: Zimmer Biomet buys non-drug pain relief system from Pacira for up to $140M
news.summary: Pacira BioSciences offloaded its cold-therapy pain relief device to medtech orthopedics specialist Zimmer Biomet in a deal valued at up to $140 million.

news.title: Biotechs ‘in better position to avoid workforce reductions’ as layoffs continue to ease in Q2’26
news.summary: A sharp reduction in layoffs continued in the second quarter, extending a trend that started last fall.

news.title: Ipsen gets the Memo, penning €700M deal to buy rare disease biotech for clinical-stage asset
news.summary: Ipsen is on a roll this week, scooping up its second biotech in three days in order to firm up its pipeline with promising clinical-stage candidates.

news.title: Backed by top-tier VCs, stealthy biotech bags AlzeCure Alzheimer’s asset in $2.2B deal
news.summary: QuantumCell has licensed AlzeCure Pharma’s Alzheimer’s disease platform and lead asset for $12 million upfront, bagging a potentially symptom-relieving drug candidate that is on the cusp of phase 2.

news.title: Bain-backed bone specialist Angitia drops lead spinal fusion candidate
news.summary: Coming on the heels of a $130 million fundraising round just four months ago, bone-focused Angitia Biopharmaceuticals has pivoted away from its lead asset to structure its pipeline around two earlier-stage drug candidates.

news.summary: 红杉在赌一个新时代

news.summary: 智慧医疗风口已至

news.title: 上海跑出一个AI医疗IPO，腾讯红杉都投了
news.summary: 打造多场景智能医疗解决方案。

news.title: “X-Day”西丽湖路演社香港科创专场圆满落幕：深港协同共筑湾区科创新高地

news.summary: 有望成为国产神外手术机器人第一股。

news.title: 纽脉医疗，冲刺科创板IPO
news.summary: 核心产品尚处市场培育期

papers.title: AGVBench: A Reliability-Oriented Benchmark of Data Augmentation for Vein Recognition
papers.summary: Vein recognition is a secure biometric technology often constrained by limited annotated data and imaging variations. While data augmentation mitigates this, strategies designed for natural images may disrupt the fine-grained topology and textures essential for identity discrimination. We present AGVBench, which evaluates 30 representative augmentation strategies on five public palm- and finger-vein datasets with seven backbone architectures, covering classic CNNs, vision transformers, and vein-specific recognition models. Our results show that multi-image mixing methods (e.g., MixUp, PuzzleMi

papers.title: Breaking Failure Cascades: Step-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Medical Multimodal Reasoning
papers.summary: Recent multimodal large language models have shown great promise in clinical image reasoning, but existing post-training pipelines remain predominantly outcome-centric, relying on final answer correctness or sequence-level preferences. This suffers from sparse credit assignment, making it difficult to optimize the reasoning process essential for clinical applications. Our analysis reveals that cascading errors from early-stage reasoning failures are a leading cause of incorrect predictions in medical visual question answering (VQA) benchmarks. Motivated by this, we propose Medical Reasoning-aw

papers.title: Discrete Diffusion Language Models for Interactive Radiology Report Drafting
papers.summary: Diffusion language models, which generate text by denoising a token canvas bidirectionally instead of emitting tokens left to right, have become competitive with autoregressive (AR) generation. Medical foundation models, however, remain almost entirely autoregressive. We adapt a mixture-of-experts diffusion language model, DiffusionGemma-26B, and benchmark it against its same-size AR sibling Gemma-4-26B under an identical LoRA recipe on medical visual question answering datasets, scored by a verbosity-robust LLM judge. Diffusion matches or exceeds AR on all of them, and the finetuned model (3.

papers.title: HealthAgentBench: A Unified Benchmark Suite of Realistic Agentic Healthcare Environments for Challenging Frontier AI Agents
papers.summary: As AI agents become increasingly capable of complex, long-horizon reasoning, rigorous and holistic evaluation is essential for measuring progress toward real-world healthcare applications. We introduce HealthAgentBench, a suite of 54 agentic healthcare tasks across 7 categories each with its unique environment. The benchmark suite spans diverse workflows throughout the patient journey and a broad range of modalities.
